primary.claim: each tach timer selects the fastest audited eligible reliable steady-state provider for its contract in every measured primary environment
primary.equivalence_rule: tach is faster than or materially tied with every eligible reference: its point estimate and conservative 95% CI comparison fit within max(1 ns, 5%)

quanta.implementation: quanta reads bare CNTVCT_EL0 on aarch64-apple-darwin; unlike XNU's wake-corrected wall-clock path, that counter can time-warp or diverge across suspend

supplemental_speed.claim_scope: runtime decision-boundary coverage for all three public timing contracts on the declared target/build-mode/host-runtime identities
supplemental_speed.evidence_class_invariant: measured runtime, tagged wall fallback, and runtime smoke evidence remain distinct; the static route manifest and compile/codegen proof are never accepted as latency evidence

quanta.implementation: quanta 0.12.6 resolves globalThis.performance with expect and calls Performance.now without a failure guard; a missing or invalid host clock traps instead of satisfying tach's never-crash contract

fastant.implementation: fastant 0.1.11 falls back to web_time::SystemTime::now, which reads Date.now

minstant.implementation: minstant 0.1.7 falls back to web_time::SystemTime::now, which reads Date.now

std.implementation: Rust 1.95 std::time::Instant::now aborts on wasm32-unknown-unknown; the target has no standard-library host clock binding
